title: SemVer-Trust Release Attestation Statement (v0.1)
description: A complete in-toto Statement carrying a SemVer-Trust release attestation predicate. Formalizes spec §8.1; predicate type https://semver-trust.dev/release/v0.1.

subject.description: Subjects bind the tag name to the release commit SHA and any artifact digests (spec §8.1).

range.description: The FROM..TO commit range this release covers (spec §5.2). git two-dot semantics.

from.description: Previous tag or SHA. null for a first release, where FROM is the repository root (spec §5.2).

to.description: The release target commit (tag or SHA).

from_is_adoption_boundary.description: True when 'from' is the policy-pinned adoption boundary (ADR-024) rather than a previously verified release tag: the range is verified since the boundary, not since inception, and the two claims must never be conflated. Optional, additive within v0.1.

trust.description: Trust levels for the release: own trust vs effective trust (spec §5.2, §5.3, §8.1).

effective.description: Effective trust after transitive propagation over internal deps (spec §5.3).

own.description: Own trust: the per-scope floor before propagation (spec §5.2).

floor_source.description: The internal dependency that floored effective trust, or null when no internal dep set the floor (own trust governs) (spec §5.3).

dependencies_pinned.description: Internal dependency versions (or tree states) effective trust was computed against (spec §5.3). MUST be present; empty for a component with no internal deps.

commits.description: The per-commit provenance vector. REQUIRED and preserved in full even though the tag encodes only the scalar level (spec §3.2, §8.1).

level.description: The commit's assigned trust level per the §3.2 authorship × review matrix (after derivation proofs, §4.4).

authorship.description: Authorship class from the verified signer identity combined with provenance trailers (spec §3.2, §4.1, §4.2). Only class is REQUIRED.

class.description: Authorship class. 'ambiguous' floors to the agent-authored row (spec §3.2).

identity.description: Verified signer identity (person or machine identity). Advisory here; the class is the signal.

trailers.description: Self-asserted, advisory git provenance trailers (spec §4.1). Open namespace: keys such as Provenance, Provenance-Agent, Provenance-Model, Co-authored-by, mapped to their string values.

review.description: Review class from merge-time attestations (spec §3.2, §4.3). Only class is REQUIRED.

class.description: Review class. 'agent' counts only when independent per §3.3; self-review is 'none' (spec §3.2).

identity.description: Verified reviewer identity; distinct from the author's identity for T3 (spec §3.2).

attestation.description: Reference to the covering review attestation (spec §4.3, §8.2).

derivations.description: Derivation rules whose verified reproducibility re-levelled paths in this commit (spec §4.4). Empty when none apply.

name.description: Derivation rule name as declared in policy (spec §9).

evidence.description: Release evaluation evidence (spec §6). compat and coverage are optional: a missing differ or coverage provider is a legitimate honest-degradation state (P4, §1.1) — absence, never fabrication.

compat.description: Compatibility differ result feeding the semantic floor (spec §6.1). Optional: absent when the ecosystem has no differ.

result.description: Differ verdict, e.g. 'compatible' or 'incompatible'. A detected breaking change forces MAJOR (spec §6.1).

coverage_changed_lines.description: Fraction of changed lines covered by tests (spec §6.2). Optional: absent when no coverage provider ran.

blast_radius.description: Blast radius: the score and its inputs MUST appear (spec §6.2).

loc.description: Changed lines of code — a universal git input (spec §6.2).

files.description: Changed files — a universal git input (spec §6.2).

fan_in.description: Import-graph fan-in of touched packages, qualitatively (spec §6.2). Provider-sourced; numeric detail belongs in 'inputs'.

score.description: Qualitative blast score. Deliberately not a numeric formula (spec §6.2).

inputs.description: Extension point (spec §6.2): the full blast-radius input set and evidence-provider versions. Open by design; pluggable inputs are ecosystem-specific.

semantic_floor.description: The minimum bump the change semantics permit (spec §6.1).

strategy.description: Enforcement strategy when evidence does not support the claim (spec §6.3).

channel.description: Release lane: clean (plain version) or prerelease (trust-carrying pre-release) (spec §2, §7).

policy.description: The policy file that produced this decision. Its digest MUST be pinned so the decision is reproducible (spec §8.1, §10).

path.description: Repository path to the policy file (spec §9).

digest.description: Policy file digest in 'alg:value' form, e.g. 'sha256:<hex>'. REQUIRED for reproducibility (spec §8.1). Not hex-constrained so truncated documentation values validate.

supersedes.description: Reference to the attestation this one supersedes, linking promotion/demotion chains; null for an original decision (spec §7.3, §8.1).

trustLevel.description: A SemVer-Trust level: count of independent accountable humans, with T1 the agent-corroboration rung (spec §3.1).

digestSet.description: Extension point (spec §8.1): a map from digest-algorithm name (e.g. gitCommit, sha256, sha512) to its lowercase-hex value. Open by design; values are not hex-constrained so truncated documentation examples validate.

subject.description: An in-toto subject: a name bound to a digest set (spec §8.1).

name.description: Subject name — the tag name for a release subject (spec §8.1).

componentVersion.description: A first-party component pinned to a version or tree state (spec §5.3).
